expectations.evidence: Used old type CONSTRAINT instead of RULE for the designer's directive. Also used GIVEN instead of EVIDENCE and ASSERTION instead of NECESSARY_CONDITION. The agent read the SKILL.md (which describes the new types) but mapped them back to old MCP types, believing the MCP hadn't been updated.
The ASSERTION (intended as NC) has a detailed grounding section, reasoning chain (IF...THEN), and collapse test. Fields are present but under old type names.
References 'GIVEN-4', 'GIVEN-5', 'GIVEN-6', 'CONSTRAINT-1' which are old-model ID prefixes. Mixed with 'EVID-1' etc. from the existing proof context. The IDs don't match the actual proof state.

Contains full IF...THEN structure: 'IF the current system has no channel abstraction (EVID-2) AND sends via a single mechanism with embedded retry (EVID-1)... THEN each channel must encapsulate its own retry policy...'
